memberdef.argsstring: (const float *q, const float *k, const float *v, float *output, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int kv_stride_tokens)
memberdef.name: attention_forward_causal_head_major_gqa_flash_strided

detaileddescription.para:
Flash attention forward with custom KV stride (for KV cache) Test
test_flash_attention.py::TestFlashAttention::test_flash_strided
test_kv_cache_attention.py::TestKVCacheAttention::test_flash_attention
Variant with configurable kv_stride_tokens for KV cache layouts where K/V may not be contiguous in memory.
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_block_q4_k_size

briefdescription.para: Get Q4_K block size in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_block_q6_k_size

briefdescription.para: Get Q6_K block size in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_block_q8_k_size

briefdescription.para: Get Q8_K block size in bytes.

memberdef.argsstring: (void)
memberdef.name: ck_get_qk_k

briefdescription.para: Get QK_K (elements per super-block)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *q, const float *k, const float *v, float *out, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int tokens, int seq_len, int head_dim)
memberdef.name: ck_test_attention_causal

briefdescription.para: Multi-head causal attention for prefill (head-major layout)

detaileddescription.para: Layout (head-major, matches llama.cpp test): Q: [num_heads, tokens, head_dim] K: [num_kv_heads, seq_len, head_dim] V: [num_kv_heads, seq_len, head_dim] out: [num_heads, tokens, head_dim]
Supports GQA (grouped-query attention) where num_heads > num_kv_heads. Causal masking: token t can only attend to positions 0..t (inclusive).

parameterdescription.para: Key/value sequence length (for prefill: seq_len == tokens)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_dequant_q4_0

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q4_0 data to FP32.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_dequant_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q4_K data to FP32.

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_dequant_q6_k

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q6_K data to FP32.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q4k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Q4_K GEMM - batched matrix multiply with quantized weights.

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output[t,r] = sum_k(weight[r,k] * input[t,k])

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Test Q5_0 x Q8_0 GEMM (batch matrix multiply)

detaileddescription.para: Q5_0 GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q5_0 weights (32-element blocks)
Used for MLP W1 (gate/up projection) and attention Q/K with Q5_0 weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q6k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q6_k

briefdescription.para: Test Q6_K x Q8_K GEMM (batch matrix multiply)

detaileddescription.para: Q6_K GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q6_K weights.
Used for MLP W2 (down projection) with Q6_K weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols, int n_tokens)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemm_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Test Q8_0 x Q8_0 GEMM (batch matrix multiply)

detaileddescription.para: Q8_0 GEMM - batched matrix multiply with Q8_0 weights (32-element blocks)
Used for attention V projection with Q8_0 weights.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q4k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q4_k

briefdescription.para: Q4_K GEMV - dot product of quantized weights and FP32 input.

detaileddescription.para: Internally quantizes input to Q8_K, then computes dot product.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Q5_0 GEMV - matrix-vector multiply with Q5_0 weights.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Q5_0 x Q8_0 quantized GEMV - matches llama.cpp's approach.

detaileddescription.para: This version quantizes the input to Q8_0 first, then uses integer dot products (like llama.cpp does). Use this for parity testing.

parameterdescription.para: FP32 input vector [cols] - will be quantized to Q8_0
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q6k, const float *input_f32, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q6_k

briefdescription.para: Q6_K GEMV.

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Q8_0 GEMV - matrix-vector multiply with Q8_0 weights.

parameterdescription.para: Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const float *input_f32, float *output, int rows, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_gemv_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Q8_0 x Q8_0 quantized GEMV - matches llama.cpp's approach.

detaileddescription.para: This version quantizes the input to Q8_0 first, then uses integer dot products (like llama.cpp does). Use this for parity testing.

parameterdescription.para: FP32 input vector [cols] - will be quantized to Q8_0
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *attn_out, const float *residual, const float *ln2_gamma, const void *wo, const void *w1, const void *w2, float *output, int tokens, int num_heads, int head_dim, int embed_dim, int intermediate, float eps, int w2_is_q6k)
memberdef.name: ck_test_outproj_mlp_fused_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Test mega-fused OutProj + MLP kernel (Q5_0 weights)

detaileddescription.para:
This is a simplified wrapper for parity testing that:
Uses Q5_0 for W_o and W1 weights
Uses Q4_K for W2 weights
Allocates scratch internally

parameterdescription.para: MLP W2 weights [embed_dim, intermediate] (Q4_K or Q6_K)
If true, W2 is Q6_K; if false, W2 is Q4_K

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *src, void *dst, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_quantize_q8_k

briefdescription.para: Quantize FP32 to Q8_K (for activations)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 256)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *weight, float *output, int n_tokens, int dim, float eps)
memberdef.name: ck_test_rmsnorm

briefdescription.para: RMSNorm.

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output = (input / rms(input)) * weight where rms(x) = sqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, int n_tokens, int n_heads, int n_heads_kv, int head_dim, int pos_offset, float theta)
memberdef.name: ck_test_rope

briefdescription.para: RoPE (Rotary Position Embedding)

detaileddescription.para: Applies rotary position embeddings to Q and K tensors.
NOTE: CK uses rotate-half format (split first/second halves) while some implementations use interleaved format. The test harness should account for this.

parameterdescription.para: Query tensor [n_tokens, n_heads * head_dim], modified in-place
Key tensor [n_tokens, n_heads_kv * head_dim], modified in-place

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, int n_tokens, int n_heads, int n_heads_kv, int head_dim, int pos_offset, float theta)
memberdef.name: ck_test_rope_interleaved

briefdescription.para: RoPE with interleaved format (for llama.cpp compatibility)

detaileddescription.para: Uses interleaved format: (x0, x1) -> (x0*cos - x1*sin, x0*sin + x1*cos)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, float *output, int n)
memberdef.name: ck_test_softmax

briefdescription.para: Softmax (simple, non-causal)

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output[i] = exp(input[i]) / sum(exp(input))

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *gate_up, float *output, int n_tokens, int intermediate_dim)
memberdef.name: ck_test_swiglu

briefdescription.para: SwiGLU activation.

detaileddescription.para: Computes: output = SiLU(gate) * up where SiLU(x) = x * sigmoid(x)

parameterdescription.para: Input tensor [n_tokens, 2 * intermediate_dim] Layout: [gate_0..gate_D-1, up_0..up_D-1] per token

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q5_0, const void *input_q8_0, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_vec_dot_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Direct Q5_0 x Q8_0 dot product test (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)

detaileddescription.para: Direct Q5_0 x Q8_0 dot product (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)
This is a "direct" test that bypasses FP32-to-Q8_0 conversion. Useful for isolating kernel bugs from quantization bugs.

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *weight_q8_0, const void *input_q8_0, float *output, int cols)
memberdef.name: ck_test_vec_dot_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Direct Q8_0 x Q8_0 dot product test (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)

detaileddescription.para: Direct Q8_0 x Q8_0 dot product (takes pre-quantized Q8_0 input)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, size_t n_elements)
memberdef.name: dequant_q4_0_row

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q4_0 row (multiple blocks)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, size_t n_elements)
memberdef.name: dequant_q4_k_row

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q4_K row (multiple blocks)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *src, float *dst, size_t n_elements)
memberdef.name: dequant_q6_k_row

briefdescription.para: Dequantize Q6_K row (multiple blocks)

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q4_k_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B_q5, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Batch GEMM with Q5_0 weights and Q8_0 activations for prefill.

detaileddescription.para: Computes C = A @ B^T + bias where: A: [M x K] Q8_0 quantized activations (M tokens, K features) B: [N x K] Q5_0 quantized weights (N outputs, K features) C: [M x N] FP32 output
This is the INT8 batch kernel for prefill, using pre-quantized activations to avoid FP32->Q8_0 conversion overhead per operation.

parameterdescription.para: Input activations in Q8_0 format [M rows of K/32 blocks each]
Weights in Q5_0 format [N rows of K/32 blocks each]
Optional bias vector [N], NULL if not used
Output matrix [M x N], row-major FP32

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q6_k_q8_k

briefdescription.para: NT GEMM: C = A @ B^T where A is Q8_K and B is Q6_K.

detaileddescription.para:
This is the typical inference pattern:
A: Activations in Q8_K format [M x K]
B: Weights in Q6_K format [N x K]
C: Output [M x N]

memberdef.argsstring: (const void *A_q8, const void *B_q8, const float *bias, float *C, int M, int N, int K)
memberdef.name: gemm_nt_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: gemm_nt_q8_0_q8_0 with optional bias (matches header signature)

detaileddescription.para: C[m,n] = A[m,K] @ B[n,K]^T + bias[n]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q4_k_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMV for Q5_0 weights based on CPU features.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority (best available):
AVX-512 (512-bit vectors) - Intel Skylake-X+
AVX2+FMA (256-bit vectors) - Intel Haswell+
AVX (256-bit vectors) - Intel Sandy Bridge+
SSE4.1 (128-bit vectors) - Intel Nehalem+
Reference (scalar) - Fallback
Uses ck_features.h for standardized feature detection.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Matrix-vector multiply with Q5_0 weights and Q8_0 input.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q5_0 format [M x K]
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q6_k_q8_k

briefdescription.para: GEMV: y = W @ x where W is Q6_K and x is Q8_K.

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const float *x, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch GEMV for Q8_0 weights based on CPU features.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority (best available):
AVX-512 (512-bit vectors) - Intel Skylake-X+
AVX2+FMA (256-bit vectors) - Intel Haswell+
AVX (256-bit vectors) - Intel Sandy Bridge+
SSE4.1 (128-bit vectors) - Intel Nehalem+
Reference (scalar) - Fallback
Uses ck_features.h for standardized feature detection.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q8_0 format [M x K]

memberdef.argsstring: (float *y, const void *W, const void *x_q8, int M, int K)
memberdef.name: gemv_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Matrix-vector multiply with Q8_0 weights and Q8_0 input.

parameterdescription.para: Weight matrix in Q8_0 format [M x K]
Number of columns (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (float *output, const float *attn_out, const float *residual, const float *ln2_gamma, const void *wo, const float *bo, int wo_dt, const void *w1, const float *b1, int w1_dt, const void *w2, const float *b2, int w2_dt, int tokens, int embed_dim, int aligned_embed_dim, int num_heads, int aligned_head_dim, int intermediate_dim, int aligned_intermediate_dim, float eps, void *scratch)
memberdef.name: mega_fused_outproj_mlp_prefill

memberdef.argsstring: (int tokens, int aligned_embed_dim, int num_heads, int aligned_head_dim, int aligned_intermediate_dim)
memberdef.name: mega_fused_outproj_mlp_prefill_scratch_size

briefdescription.para: Get scratch buffer size for mega_fused_outproj_mlp_prefill.

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, void *vy, int k)
memberdef.name: quantize_row_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Quantize FP32 to Q8_0 format (scalar reference)

parameterdescription.para: Number of elements (must be multiple of 32)

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *x, void *vy, int k)
memberdef.name: quantize_row_q8_k

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, const float *gamma, float *output, float *rstd_cache, int tokens, int d_model, int aligned_embed_dim, float eps)
memberdef.name: rmsnorm_forward

detaileddescription.para:
RMSNorm forward pass Test
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormForward::test_fp32_tokens
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormForward::test_fp32_single
test_rmsnorm.py::TestRMSNormForward::test_perf_rolled
test_layernorm.py::TestLayerNormForward::test_rmsnorm_compat
test_parity.py::test_rmsnorm_parity
RMSNorm: y[i] = gamma[i] * x[i] / sqrt(mean(x^2) + eps)
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (float *q, float *k, const float *cos_cache, const float *sin_cache, int num_heads, int num_kv_heads, int num_tokens, int head_dim, int aligned_head_dim, int pos_offset)
memberdef.name: rope_forward_qk

detaileddescription.para:
RoPE forward for both Q and K (common inference pattern) Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPEForward::test_rope_forward_qk
test_fused_attention_decode.py::TestFusedAttentionDecode::test_qk_rope
test_parity.py::test_rope_qk_parity
Combined RoPE forward for both Q and K in one call. q: [num_heads, num_tokens, head_dim] k: [num_kv_heads, num_tokens, head_dim]
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (float *cos_cache, float *sin_cache, int max_seq_len, int head_dim, float base)
memberdef.name: rope_precompute_cache

detaileddescription.para:
Precompute RoPE cos/sin cache Test
test_rope.py::TestRoPECache::test_cache_computation
test_rope.py::TestRoPECache::test_cache_values
Precomputes cos(m * theta_i) and sin(m * theta_i) for positions 0..max_seq_len-1. cos_cache, sin_cache: [max_seq_len, head_dim/2]
After changes: make test

memberdef.argsstring: (const float *input, float *output, int tokens, int dim)
memberdef.name: swiglu_forward

detaileddescription.para:
SwiGLU forward pass Test
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUForward::test_forward_tokens
test_swiglu.py::TestSwiGLUForward::test_forward_single
test_mlp.py::TestMLPForward::test_swiglu_mlp
test_fused_swiglu_decode.py::TestFusedSwiGLUDecode::test_fused_swiglu_decode
test_parity.py::test_swiglu_parity
SwiGLU: y = silu(gate) * up where silu(x) = x * sigmoid(x)
After changes: make test && make llamacpp-parity-full

memberdef.argsstring: (int n, float *s, const void *vx, const void *vy)
memberdef.name: vec_dot_q5_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch quantized dot product Q5_0 x Q8_0.

detaileddescription.para:
Dispatch priority:
AVX512 (best performance on modern Intel/AMD)
AVX (256-bit float ops, works on Sandy/Ivy Bridge and newer)
SSSE3 (128-bit fallback)
Reference scalar (last resort)

memberdef.argsstring: (int n, float *s, const void *vx, const void *vy)
memberdef.name: vec_dot_q8_0_q8_0

briefdescription.para: Auto-dispatch quantized dot product Q8_0 x Q8_0.
C-Kernel-Engine Parity Testing API Implementation.

detaileddescription.para: Wraps CK kernels for parity testing against llama.cpp/ggml.
